Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bind package, non-root container, health, migration ownership, compatibility, canary, and rollback evidence; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Run migrations through one release-owned step with locking, timeout, version compatibility, evidence, and explicit failure recovery.
